title: vendor in containers/(common,buildah,storage)
body:
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

eep. Is there something in particular you'd like me to look at? The only thing that springs to mind is that if we're truly done with go 1.16, maybe this line can be removed:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/181c9d3ee3ad4820b9ae91f1f02faf7b9e65fc87/go.mod#L78

Oh..... I get it now. I was looking for buildah test changes that would cause the `bud` test to fail -- having forgotten that the `tests` dir is not vendored.

OK. Buildah bud tests are going to fail, and fail early (in the patching stage). I would be most grateful if you would, in your checked-out directory for this branch, run:
```
$ hack/buildah-vendor-treadmill --pick
```
...and post the log here, whether it works or not (if it fails, so I can debug it; if it works, for posterity). Thank you!

$ ./hack/buildah-vendor-treadmill --pick
Can't locate open.pm in @INC (you may need to install the open module) (@INC contains: /usr/local/lib64/perl5/5.34 /usr/local/share/perl5/5.34 /usr/lib64/perl5/vendor_perl /usr/share/perl5/vendor_perl /usr/lib64/perl5 /usr/share/perl5) at ./hack/buildah-vendor-treadmill line 9.
BEGIN failed--compilation aborted at ./hack/buildah-vendor-treadmill line 9.

Ouch - I'm so sorry, I thought that was a standard perl package. It's not. Would you be okay with:
```console
# dnf install perl-open perl-File-Temp perl-JSON perl-libwww-perl
```
If that's too much of a hassle, you could try a local checkout of #13808 and cherry-picking the first (ONLY THE FIRST) commit.

Already grabbed it and added to this commit.

After installing perl-open, I am seeing.

$ ./hack/buildah-vendor-treadmill --pick
|
+---> HEAD is a buildah vendor commit. Good.
501 Protocol scheme 'https' is not supported (LWP::Protocol::https not installed)

Thanks. Looks like I need to re-think the `--pick` step, maybe do it in bash instead, at the cost of some error checking. For now, I think you should be OK with the approach you used (manually merging the changes). Sorry about the hassle.
If this works, that is the least hassle I have ever had.

I ran `test/buildah-bud/run-buildah-bud-tests` on my laptop. Patches applied cleanly (yay), tests mostly passed. The two exceptions were of the form:
```
  STEP 2/2: RUN apk add nginx
   fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
   ERROR: http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/main: temporary error (try again later)
```
which is an unfortunate flake we get periodically.

Alas, I've just realized that this PR is based on 6984a0f35, and does not include #13990 (the remote --tls-verify fix) so `bud remote` is going to fail. Sorry about that, I wish I'd caught it sooner.

LGTM
once you win the thumb wrestling match with the tests.

Yep, `bud remote` failed. Easiest solution: rebase on latest main, and repush. (The good news is that `bud local` passed, and `bud remote` only failed in the single test I was expecting to fail. IOW, the treadmill process is working).
